steps.title: Identify the boundary line
steps.content: From the graph, the boundary line passes through $(0,6)$ and $(1.5,0)$. Slope:$$m=\frac{0-6}{1.5-0}=-4$$With y-intercept $6$, the boundary is $y=-4x+6$.

steps.title: Determine the inequality direction
steps.content: The shaded region lies above the line. Test a point clearly above, say $(0,8)$: is $8>-4(0)+6=6$? Yes. So the inequality is $y>-4x+6$.

steps.title: Eliminate the answer choices
steps.content:
• A) $y<-4x+6$: correct line but the shading is above, not below.
  • B) $y<6x-4$: slope $6$ and y-intercept $-4$ do not match the boundary.
  • C) $y>-4x+6$: correct line and correct direction (above).
  • D) $y>6x-4$: wrong slope and wrong y-intercept.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
